text: Supermarket data from Circana shows volume growth in food and beverages of 1.1 percent through April compared to the same period in 2024. metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text: Until April 2025, the prices of food have increased by 3.5 percent and of non-alcoholic drinks by 8.8 percent. The price increase of alcoholic beverages was limited at 1.8 percent. In the recent Economic Survey of the Netherlands (COEN), entrepreneurs in the food industry have a more positive view of turnover and profit expectations in April of this year than a few months earlier. Half of beverage entrepreneurs still cite insufficient demand as a major obstacle. The proposed simplification of the Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (CSRD) and Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) will relieve workload for many companies in the sector in the short term.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text: Every year, the Dutch beverage and food industry exports more than 2.3 billion euros to the United States (US). This amounts to approximately 3 percent of total exports. The Netherlands mainly exports alcoholic beverages, fish, crustaceans & molluscs, vegetables & fruit, cheese and chocolate to the US. Earlier this year, ABN AMRO made an analysis of import duties. This shows that exports to the US could fall by 23 percent with a tariff of 10 percent.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text: This amounts to approximately 3 percent of total exports. The Netherlands mainly exports alcoholic beverages, fish, crustaceans & molluscs, vegetables & fruit, cheese and chocolate to the US. Earlier this year, ABN AMRO made an analysis of import duties. This shows that exports to the US could fall by 23 percent with a tariff of 10 percent. Individual companies with a lot of exports to the US and less ability to ship products to other markets could be hit hard.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text: The Netherlands mainly exports alcoholic beverages, fish, crustaceans & molluscs, vegetables & fruit, cheese and chocolate to the US. Earlier this year, ABN AMRO made an analysis of import duties. This shows that exports to the US could fall by 23 percent with a tariff of 10 percent. Individual companies with a lot of exports to the US and less ability to ship products to other markets could be hit hard. Food companies with long-term relationships say they can share the pain among themselves, traders are more likely to opt to stop trading.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text:
Earlier this year, ABN AMRO made an analysis of import duties. This shows that exports to the US could fall by 23 percent with a tariff of 10 percent. Individual companies with a lot of exports to the US and less ability to ship products to other markets could be hit hard. Food companies with long-term relationships say they can share the pain among themselves, traders are more likely to opt to stop trading. Some of the companies are currently protected by contractual agreements in which their selling prices are fixed. [Read also: Food exports to the US halve due to Trump import tariff](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/food/export-food-to-us-halveert-due-import Tariff-trump.html)
Import tariffs loom again for specific products.

Import tariffs loom again for specific products. The European Union (EU) has announced that previously suspended countermeasures in response to 2018 US import tariffs will be reinstated as part of a possible counter-reaction. This concerns strategically chosen products, including corn, rice, cranberries and cranberry juice, whiskey (bourbon), orange juice and dried beans.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

Import tariffs loom again for specific products. The European Union (EU) has announced that previously suspended countermeasures in response to 2018 US import tariffs will be reinstated as part of a possible counter-reaction. This concerns strategically chosen products, including corn, rice, cranberries and cranberry juice, whiskey (bourbon), orange juice and dried beans. In 2024, approximately 2.1 billion worth of food and drinks from the US crossed the Dutch border.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

Import tariffs loom again for specific products. The European Union (EU) has announced that previously suspended countermeasures in response to 2018 US import tariffs will be reinstated as part of a possible counter-reaction. This concerns strategically chosen products, including corn, rice, cranberries and cranberry juice, whiskey (bourbon), orange juice and dried beans. In 2024, approximately 2.1 billion worth of food and drinks from the US crossed the Dutch border. Vegetables, fruit and nuts and alcoholic beverages in particular represent a high value for which possible countermeasures apply.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text: The European Union (EU) has announced that previously suspended countermeasures in response to 2018 US import tariffs will be reinstated as part of a possible counter-reaction. This concerns strategically chosen products, including corn, rice, cranberries and cranberry juice, whiskey (bourbon), orange juice and dried beans. In 2024, approximately 2.1 billion worth of food and drinks from the US crossed the Dutch border. Vegetables, fruit and nuts and alcoholic beverages in particular represent a high value for which possible countermeasures apply. About 8 percent of imported beef from the US remains free of import tariffs for the time being.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text: This concerns strategically chosen products, including corn, rice, cranberries and cranberry juice, whiskey (bourbon), orange juice and dried beans. In 2024, approximately 2.1 billion worth of food and drinks from the US crossed the Dutch border. Vegetables, fruit and nuts and alcoholic beverages in particular represent a high value for which possible countermeasures apply. About 8 percent of imported beef from the US remains free of import tariffs for the time being. Beef imports are made under strict conditions within a fixed quota for which no tariffs apply.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text: In 2024, approximately 2.1 billion worth of food and drinks from the US crossed the Dutch border. Vegetables, fruit and nuts and alcoholic beverages in particular represent a high value for which possible countermeasures apply. About 8 percent of imported beef from the US remains free of import tariffs for the time being. Beef imports are made under strict conditions within a fixed quota for which no tariffs apply. Companies in the US are struggling to fully utilize this quota because the EU has strict regulations, including the ban on growth hormones and the European deforestation regulation EUDR.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

text: Vegetables, fruit and nuts and alcoholic beverages in particular represent a high value for which possible countermeasures apply. About 8 percent of imported beef from the US remains free of import tariffs for the time being. Beef imports are made under strict conditions within a fixed quota for which no tariffs apply. Companies in the US are struggling to fully utilize this quota because the EU has strict regulations, including the ban on growth hormones and the European deforestation regulation EUDR. In anticipation of possible countermeasures, some parties are cautious about placing large orders, while other parties are building up large stocks of non-perishable products in particular.

metadata.source_title: International chains increase unpredictability in the food industry

In anticipation of possible countermeasures, some parties are cautious about placing large orders, while other parties are building up large stocks of non-perishable products in particular. Companies in the US are struggling to fully utilize this quota because the EU has strict regulations, including the ban on growth hormones and the European deforestation regulation EUDR. In anticipation of possible countermeasures, some parties are cautious about placing large orders, while other parties are building up large stocks of non-perishable products in particular.
